mnemonics.title: Interrupt Vector Addresses
mnemonics.content: Mnemonic: 'Everyone Takes Interesting Courses' - Ext0(0003), Timer0(000B), Int1(0013), Timer1(001B), Serial(0023). The pattern: +8 each for timer, +16 for int1, +24 for timer1, +32 for serial.

mnemonics.title: IE Register Bits
mnemonics.content: Mnemonic: 'EA X X ES ET1 EX1 ET0 EX0' - Enable All, then Enable Serial, Timer1, External1, Timer0, External0 (all from high to low bits).

mnemonics.title: IP Register Bits
mnemonics.content: Mnemonic: 'PS PT1 PX1 PT0 PX0' - Priority Serial, Timer1, External1, Timer0, External0. Undefined bits are X.

mnemonics.title: Stack Save Order
mnemonics.content: Remember: ISR saves ACC → PSW → B → DPTR → Registers. On RETI: DPTR → B → PSW → ACC (reverse order).

acronyms.usage: Controls enabling/disabling of individual interrupts in 8051. Bits: EA, -, ES, ET1, EX1, ET0, EX0.
Sets high (1) or low (0) priority for each interrupt source. Higher priority can interrupt lower.
Special function that executes when interrupt occurs. Must end with RETI instruction.
Pops PC from stack, restores interrupt system, enables nested interrupts. Must be used in ISR.
Also controls external interrupt types (IT0, IT1) and flags (IE0, IE1).
Signal line that signals CPU to suspend current execution and handle interrupt event.

visualTips.visualization: Draw a memory map showing 0000H-002AH range. Mark: 0000H-0002H (reset), 0003H-000AH (INT0), 000BH-0012H (T0), 0013H-001AH (INT1), 001BH-0022H (T1), 0023H-002AH (Serial). Notice 8-byte spacing between vectors.
Draw 8-bit register: bit7(EA)|bit6(X)|bit5(X)|bit4(ES)|bit3(ET1)|bit2(EX1)|bit1(ET0)|bit0(EX0). Put 'EA' at leftmost (MSB) as master switch.
Show stack growing upward in memory. When interrupt: push low byte of PC first, then high byte. SP increments by 2. Visual: [PCL]→[PCH] with SP pointing to PCH.
Pyramid shape: Top = INT0 (highest), below = Timer0, then INT1, then Timer1, bottom = Serial (lowest). Use numbered levels 1-5.

analogies.analogy: Like a fire alarm (interrupt) vs a security guard checking every room (polling). Alarm gets immediate attention; guard wastes time checking empty rooms.
Main program is normal daily work. Interrupt is emergency - ISR drops everything, handles crisis (saves critical state), returns to normal work.
Like taking notes before answering phone - you jot down where you were (PC), what you were doing (registers), finish call, then restore your place.
In an ER, heart attack (high priority) gets treated before broken arm (low priority). IP register assigns these priorities to different interrupt sources.
EA is like the main circuit breaker in a house. Individual interrupt enables are room switches. All lights off if main is off.
